name: Sillage

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Patch release: correct minAppVersion floor.

## Fix
- `minAppVersion` raised from 1.8.0 → **1.8.7**. The Obsidian community-plugin lint flagged `Notice.messageEl` as newer than 1.8.0; the API was actually added in 1.8.7 (per the `@since` tag in `obsidian-api`).

No behavior changes — purely a manifest correction.

## Supply chain
Release artifacts carry a signed SLSA build-provenance attestation. Verify with:
```sh
gh attestation verify main.js -R J3F3H/sillage
```

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Patch release covering a second pass of the Obsidian community-plugin review.

## Fixes
- Top-level settings heading removed. The previous "Sillage settings" heading was flagged for containing both the plugin name and the word "settings" — both are redundant since Obsidian already labels the settings tab with the plugin name.
- `minAppVersion` raised to **1.8.0** so that `Notice.messageEl` (used by the cancel-on-click handler in one-shot note commands) lines up with the declared minimum.

## Supply chain
- Release artifacts (`main.js`, `styles.css`) carry a signed SLSA build-provenance attestation as in 0.2.1. Verify with:
  ```sh
  gh attestation verify main.js -R J3F3H/sillage
  ```

Upgrading from 0.2.1 is purely a metadata + cosmetic update; no behavior changes.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Hardening release driven by the Obsidian community-plugin review. No new features; the **Reload skills** command is the only user-visible addition.

## User-facing
- Skill auto-discovery now subscribes to Obsidian vault events instead of `fs.watch`. Cross-platform (works on Linux), no more `fs.watch({recursive:true})` limitation. Added a **Reload skills** command as an explicit fallback for vaults where `.agents/` isn't indexed.
- `minAppVersion` raised to **1.7.2** so the awaited `Workspace.revealLeaf` signature matches the running Obsidian.

## Internals / review fixes
- Command id no longer carries the redundant `sillage-` prefix.
- Settings heading rendered via `new Setting().setHeading()` instead of a raw `<h2>`.
- Notice click target switched from deprecated `noticeEl` to `messageEl`.
- All `setTimeout`/`clearTimeout` calls routed through `window.*` for popout-window compatibility.
- `MarkdownRenderer.render` is now tied to the chat view's component lifecycle (was leaking under the plugin's lifetime).
- Floating `Promise<void>` calls in event handlers explicitly voided.
- Subprocess data callbacks and vibe session-meta JSON typed; no more `any` propagation.
- `builtin-modules` dependency replaced with the native `node:module.builtinModules`.

## Supply chain
- Release artifacts (`main.js`, `styles.css`) now carry a signed SLSA build-provenance attestation. Verify with:
  ```sh
  gh attestation verify main.js -R J3F3H/sillage
  ```

## Host access disclosures
The README now has a dedicated **Host access (Obsidian review disclosures)** section explicitly enumerating the three flagged behaviors:
- `child_process.spawn` for the user-configured `vibe` binary.
- `fs.promises` read of `$VIBE_HOME/logs/session/*/meta.json` (the only path Sillage touches outside the vault).
- `navigator.clipboard.writeText` from the explicit Copy button on assistant bubbles.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
First public release.

## Features

- **Chat panel** — streams `vibe --output streaming` into a sidebar view, with cancel button, tool-call visibility, and Insert / Copy buttons on assistant bubbles
- **Persisted chat history + safe session resume** — chat survives Obsidian reloads, subsequent turns use `vibe --resume <session_id>` so terminal vibe sessions can't collide; falls back fresh if the session has aged out of `$VIBE_HOME/logs/session/`
- **Note commands** — Summarize, Extract action items (Obsidian Tasks plugin syntax), Rewrite selection, Translate selection (English / French); each cancellable via the persistent Notice
- **Skill auto-discovery** — any `user-invocable: true` skill under `<vault>/.agents/skills/` registers as a command; `fs.watch` hot-reloads new/edited skills on macOS and Windows
- **Editor context menu** — right-click in any note for the selection / note commands
- **Cost / turn / duration telemetry** pulled from vibe's per-session `meta.json`
- **Configurable Agent** (`accept-edits` default, `auto-approve`, or `default`) to control vibe's tool-use approval
- **Subprocess hardening** — stdin closed, `PATH` augmented for `~/.local/bin`, in-flight processes killed on plugin unload, per-send timeout
- **Debug logging toggle** for `[sillage]` console output

## Requirements

- Desktop Obsidian 1.5.0+
- [Mistral Vibe CLI](https://docs.mistral.ai/mistral-vibe/introduction) installed and authenticated (`vibe --setup`)

## Install (manual)

See the [README](https://github.com/J3F3H/sillage#install-manual) for clone-and-symlink instructions.

## Privacy

Note content is sent to Mistral via `vibe` whenever a command runs. See [Privacy / data flow](https://github.com/J3F3H/sillage#privacy--data-flow) in the README for the full breakdown.
